plan_summary.plan_name: Automated Paperclip Manufacturing and Fulfillment Pilot
plan_summary.primary_goal: Demonstrate end-to-end autonomous paperclip production from API call to staged parcel with documented manual intervention under 2 hours per week, inside a $300k-$500k budget under the 'Builder' strategy.
plan_summary.modelling_frame: Tests whether the named hardware, integration, and expert-commissioning components plus a 10% contingency fit under the $500k cap, whether the realised on-site expert contract clears the mandatory $15k floor and stays under the $25k SMART contract cap, and whether the realised manual-intervention load stays under the 2-hour autonomy gate.

key_values.label: Total program budget ceiling
key_values.comment: Upper bound of the stated $300k-$500k range; breach threshold for the budget gate. Tested by budget_surplus_usd.

key_values.label: Manual intervention gate (autonomy)
key_values.comment: 120-minute weekly cap on manual work; the headline feasibility gate. Tested by manual_intervention_surplus.
key_values.source_text: Manual intervention permitted: under 2 hours per week.

key_values.label: Mandatory on-site expert allocation floor
key_values.comment: Issue-3 floor: at least 1 week of on-site PLC expert time must be funded post-rigging. Tested by expert_floor_margin_usd.
key_values.source_text: Allocate minimum fixed budget of $15,000 for 1 week of dedicated on-site expert time.

key_values.label: On-site expert contract cap
key_values.comment: Data Collection SMART objective: signed contract must come in below this cap. Tested by expert_cap_margin_usd.
key_values.source_text: Secure a signed contract for on-site PLC integration support with confirmed cost less than $25,000.

key_values.label: Outsourced OPC UA middleware allocation
key_values.comment: Q3 assumption: ~$50k of integration budget for the OPC UA middleware abstraction layer. Component of the bottom-up project cost.
key_values.source_text: OPC UA middleware development budget assumption is approximately $50,000.

key_values.label: Used wire bender allocation (midpoint of $20k-$40k)
key_values.comment: Midpoint of the stated $20k-$40k allocation; component of the bottom-up project cost.
key_values.source_text: Wire bending machine budget range: $20,000 to $40,000.

key_values.label: Packing machine allocation (midpoint of $10k-$30k)
key_values.comment: Midpoint of the stated $10k-$30k allocation; component of the bottom-up project cost.

key_values.label: Operational contingency fraction of budget
key_values.comment: Risk-3 action: 10% contingency on the bottom-up component sum.
key_values.source_text: Establish a 10% contingency fund; closely monitor expenditures.

derived_questions.question: Does the realised on-site expert allocation clear the $15k Issue-3 floor?
derived_questions.why_it_matters: Threshold pairing for onsite_expert_floor_usd: dropping below the floor reverts the integration posture to remote-only, which the source flags as inadequate for legacy PLC debugging.

missing_values_to_estimate.label: Realised manual-intervention hours per operational week
missing_values_to_estimate.why_needed: Drives the autonomy-gate margin against the 2-hour cap.
missing_values_to_estimate.suggested_estimation_method: Pilot log over a 4-week stabilisation window after expert engagement; sensitivity from Issue 2 (10% material variance can reduce 2-hour goal probability 50-70%).

missing_values_to_estimate.label: Realised on-site expert contract cost
missing_values_to_estimate.why_needed: Tested against the $15k floor and the $25k cap; also a component of the bottom-up project cost.
missing_values_to_estimate.suggested_estimation_method: Firm quotes from specialist PLC integrators with 1-week mobilisation; cross-check against the $7k-$12k emergency rate from Issue 3.

missing_values_to_estimate.label: Permits, OSHA compliance, and safety-interlock cost
missing_values_to_estimate.why_needed: Component of the bottom-up project cost; the source does not name a separate allocation.
missing_values_to_estimate.suggested_estimation_method: Third-party regulatory consultant quote during Phase 1; cross-check against the Risk-1 $10k-$20k cost-increase band.

missing_values_to_estimate.label: Rigging, install, and physical-integration labour cost
missing_values_to_estimate.why_needed: Component of the bottom-up project cost; Expert Criticism flags integration labour for Phases 3 and 5 as underbudgeted.
missing_values_to_estimate.suggested_estimation_method: Fixed-price rigging quotes for the 4,000 sq ft pilot cell after equipment selection.

missing_values_to_estimate.label: Internal developer / control-software labour cost
missing_values_to_estimate.why_needed: Component of the bottom-up project cost; Risk 4 names single-developer bottleneck risk.
missing_values_to_estimate.suggested_estimation_method: Loaded FTE rate times planned developer-weeks across the 6-phase plan.

recommended_first_calculations.label: Sum of named project components (before contingency)
recommended_first_calculations.formula_hint: subtotal_named_components_usd = wire_bender_midpoint_usd + packing_machine_midpoint_usd + opc_ua_middleware_cost_usd + actual_onsite_expert_cost_usd + permits_and_safety_cost_usd + rigging_and_install_cost_usd + internal_developer_cost_usd
recommended_first_calculations.why_first: Bottom-up sum of named draws against the cap; preserves each named component as a primitive instead of collapsing into a flat total.

recommended_first_calculations.label: Bottom-up project cost with contingency
recommended_first_calculations.why_first: Applies the 10% Risk-3 contingency to the named-component sum; tested against the $500k cap.

recommended_first_calculations.label: Budget headroom against the $500k cap
recommended_first_calculations.why_first: Primary budget-breach gate; positive means the bottom-up estimate fits inside the cap.

recommended_first_calculations.label: Manual-intervention margin against the autonomy gate
recommended_first_calculations.why_first: Primary autonomy-feasibility gate; positive means the pilot meets the headline feasibility goal.

recommended_first_calculations.label: On-site expert contract margin against the $25k cap
recommended_first_calculations.why_first: Threshold pairing for the $25k SMART contract cap; positive means contract fits inside the named ceiling.

unmodelled_gates.label: Building, electrical, and OSHA permits granted in Phase 1
unmodelled_gates.why_it_matters: Phase 2+ cannot begin until permits are in hand; failure halts physical install regardless of any financial gate.
unmodelled_gates.consequence_if_false: Project blocked at Phase 1; sunk costs on design and pre-procurement without operational continuation.

unmodelled_gates.label: Formal OPC UA vs. custom-I/O selection signed off by D+10
unmodelled_gates.why_it_matters: Without an architecture decision the integration timeline cannot start; binary architect sign-off the model cannot simulate.
unmodelled_gates.consequence_if_false: Control System Protocol Selection blocked; software phases stall; scope creep for the internal developer.

unmodelled_gates.label: Selected wire bender supports a documented fieldbus protocol by D+14
unmodelled_gates.why_it_matters: Premortem stops the purchase if the chosen machine lacks fieldbus support; integration then becomes a custom-driver gamble the plan refuses to take.
unmodelled_gates.consequence_if_false: Purchase blocked; alternative bender must be sourced, or strategy pivots to a custom-I/O posture with budget implications.

unmodelled_gates.label: Carrier accepts fixed-table staging for the Phase 6 demo
unmodelled_gates.why_it_matters: Phase 6 demo depends on carrier acceptance of fixed-table mechanical handoff rather than dynamic conveyor staging; binary external decision.
unmodelled_gates.consequence_if_false: Phase 6 demonstration fails; mechanical handoff must be re-engineered with dynamic staging at additional CAPEX.
